Fix SSH key negotiation error for legacy iOS devices

Add -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa to all SSH/SCP commands to support
legacy iOS devices that only offer ssh-rsa and ssh-dss key types.
Modern SSH clients have disabled these by default for security, but
legacy jailbroken iOS devices require this support.

Fixes: 'no matching host key type found' errors when connecting to
older iOS devices over SSH/SCP.

﻿network: fix zombie kick not closing TCP connection

After kick_peer() the registry drops its clone of writer_tx, but
PeerConnection still holds its own clone. Since mpsc::UnboundedSender
only closes when ALL clones are dropped, the I/O bridge task stays alive,
the TCP socket stays open, and the zombie's message loop keeps running --
continuing to send GetBlocks requests that spawn spawn_blocking tasks and
contend for locks, causing RPC stalls.

Fix: return Err("DISCONNECT: zombie peer kicked") immediately after
kick_peer(). run_message_loop_unified already breaks on "DISCONNECT:"
errors, which drops PeerConnection (and its writer_tx clone), closing the
channel, exiting the I/O bridge task, and sending TCP FIN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/stuff (#69)

* fix(usbd480): pass non-NULL buffer for zero-length OUT control transfers

WinUsb_ControlTransfer on a per-interface WinUSB composite device rejects
a NULL buffer pointer for OUT transfers even when BufferLength = 0. Despite
what MSDN documentation states, in practice WinUSB's composite interface
handling requires a valid (non-NULL) pointer for all OUT control transfers.

This matches libusb's behavior in winusbx_submit_control_transfer, which
always passes transfer->buffer + LIBUSB_CONTROL_SETUP_SIZE regardless of
whether the data length is zero.

Root cause investigation: SimHub uses USBD480_lib2.dll which embeds libusb-1.0
(WinUSB backend). libusb works because it always passes a non-NULL buffer.
Our code passed NULL + 0 for all controlOut calls (SET_ADDRESS, SET_BRIGHTNESS,
SET_FRAME_START_ADDRESS), causing every OUT transfer to fail with:
'A device attached to the system is not functioning.'

Fix: pass &dummy[0] (1-byte stack variable) as buffer with BufferLength=0.

The existing per-interface WinUSB install (Zadig -> Interface 0) is correct
and will now work without reinstalling anything.

Also updated SKILL.md with correct root cause analysis and revised Zadig
guidance: Interface 0 installs are the preferred approach (keeps HID
touchscreen active), whole-device installs also work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(usbd480): wake device from USB selective suspend on open

When SimHub disables the USBD480 screen and releases its handle, the WinUSB
driver leaves the device in AUTO_SUSPEND mode. WinUsb_Initialize succeeds
(the interface can be opened) but all subsequent control transfers fail with
ERROR_GEN_FAILURE because the USB device is still suspended at the bus level.

Fix: call WinUsb_SetPowerPolicy(AUTO_SUSPEND=FALSE) immediately after
WinUsb_Initialize. Per the Windows docs, setting AUTO_SUSPEND=FALSE on a
suspended device causes the host to bring it back to full power immediately,
making all subsequent transfers work.

Also adds procWinUsbSetPowerPolicy to the shared WinUSB proc declarations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(usbd480): use RECIP_DEVICE (0x40/0xC0) for NX series control transfers

The NX series firmware (PID 0x08A7) expects USB_RECIP_DEVICE in bmRequestType,
not USB_RECIP_INTERFACE like the old WQ43 (PID 0x08A6). Sending RECIP_INTERFACE
(0x41/0xC1) causes the NX firmware to STALL the endpoint immediately, which
Windows reports as ERROR_GEN_FAILURE (31) on every single control transfer —
both IN and OUT — regardless of the buffer or other parameters.

Confirmed via diagnostic test program:
  0xC1 (IN|VENDOR|IFACE)  → FAILED for GET_DEVICE_DETAILS
  0xC0 (IN|VENDOR|DEVICE) → OK: returns 'USBD480-NX50' + 800x480
  0x41 (OUT|VENDOR|IFACE) → FAILED for SET_BRIGHTNESS
  0x40 (OUT|VENDOR|DEVICE)→ OK

Fix: usbd480ReqTypeOut 0x41→0x40, usbd480ReqTypeIn 0xC1→0xC0

Also updates SKILL.md with the confirmed NX bmRequestType values and adds
the RECIP_INTERFACE failure mode to the Common Failure Modes table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(usbd480): add NX series support with diagnostic logging

- Target NX series (PID 0x08A7, 800x480) as the supported USBD480 model
- Update default dimensions (480x272 -> 800x480) and preset naming
- Add diagnostic logging in core.go: log when layout is (or isn't) assigned
  to each device at startup so blank-screen root cause is visible in logs
- Add diagnostic logging in base_driver.go: log painter creation with
  layout id + idle widget count, or warn when painter has no layout
- Fix ColBg comment corruption in colors.go; restore #0a0a0a value
- Replace 'SimHub' references with 'Ref' in comments and error messages
- Update skills and docs to reflect NX-only focus

Note: USBD480 idle page still not rendering (VoCore works). Diagnostic
logs added to identify root cause — needs further investigation after
observing logs with this build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: quote parameter expansions in env blocks to handle YAML special chars

When ADO expands ${{ parameters.X }} inline into a YAML env: value without
quotes, special characters in the secret (colons, tildes, hashes, braces, etc.)
can cause the YAML parser to misinterpret the value — resulting in a truncated
or corrupted string reaching the bash script.

Fix: wrap all 11 parameter expansions in YAML double quotes so the expanded
value is always parsed as a plain string regardless of its content.

Also adds debug echo of secret/PAT length (no value) to confirm correct
values arrive in bash.

Bump SkiaSharp to Skia milestone 132 (#3560)

Bump SkiaSharp to Skia milestone 132 (#3560)

Context: https://github.com/mono/skia/pull/171
Changes: https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3560/files
AI review report: https://gistcdn.githack.com/mattleibow/b1c57a78d86cc5dec3fff1182de48f01/raw/295bb903e36917d3faf80f74ba4d3ab06adcb6ec/171.html

Update SkiaSharp from Skia m119 to m132 — the managed-side companion
to the native Skia bump merged in mono/skia#171. This moves the
assembly version from 3.119.x to 3.132.0, the native soname from
119.0.0 to 132.0.0, and all NuGet package versions from 3.119.4 to
3.132.0.

~~ Why take this bump ~~

Between m119 and m132, upstream Skia gained improvements that flow
directly through to SkiaSharp users: more consistent EXIF/orientation
handling in image codecs, HDR and wide-gamut pipeline improvements,
newer color-aware filters and drop-shadows, and accumulated
raster/perlin/filter correctness fixes. This also unblocks exposing
newer Skia APIs in future releases.

~~ New API surface ~~

SKColorType gains two values for upstream HDR color types:

  * Bgra10101010XR = 25
  * RgbF16F16F16x = 26

These are wired through EnumMappings, the bytes-per-pixel and
shift-per-pixel tables in Definitions.cs, the alpha-type validation
logic, and the GL sized-format mapping in GRDefinitions.cs.

~~ Struct ABI changes ~~

  * SKJpegEncoderOptions: now includes fOrigin / fHasOrigin fields
    matching the upstream EXIF orientation support added in m132.
  * GRVkBackendContextNative and GrVkYcbcrConversionInfo: updated
    to match upstream Vulkan shape changes.

~~ Generated bindings ~~

SkiaApi.generated.cs was regenerated to match the updated C API
(174 lines changed). This includes the new color type enum values,
the Vulkan struct layout changes, and adjusted P/Invoke signatures.

~~ Native build scripts ~~

Removed skia_use_sfntly=false from all 8 platform build.cake files
(Android, iOS, Linux, macOS, Tizen, tvOS, WASM, Windows). sfntly was
removed from upstream Skia between m119 and m132; the flag is now
unrecognized.

Added GIT_SYNC_DEPS_SKIP_EMSDK=1 environment variable passthrough
in native-shared.cake's RunPython helper so that git-sync-deps does
not attempt to activate emsdk during non-WASM builds.

~~ Submodule and branch tracking ~~

  * externals/skia now points to e2260f39c4 on the skiasharp branch
    (post mono/skia#171 merge).
  * .gitmodules branch updated from xamarin-mobile-bindings to
    skiasharp, reflecting the consolidated branch name used by
    the mono/skia fork going forward.

~~ Test updates ~~

  * New m132 color types added to EnumMappingsTest and
    ToGlSizedFormat unknown-format assertions.
  * SKImage: updated refcount expectation after FromEncodedData
    (3 → 2, matching m132 internal refcount change).
  * SKColorSpace: switched to Assert.InRange for non-deterministic
    refcounts from peek.Info.
  * SKPaint/SKFont: handle woff2 typefaces returning non-null with
    empty FamilyName under m132.
  * SKBitmap: skip Bgra10101010Xr in copy tests (unsupported pixel
    color readback on current test platforms).
  * Gray8: corrected luminance values to 0x35/0x23 matching actual
    m132 HIGHP raster pipeline output.
  * GRContext: added null-check guard for Vulkan backend test.

~~ Other changes ~~

  * LoongArch Linux build mapping: loongarch64 → loong64 to match
    Skia GN's expected target_cpu.
  * VERSIONS.txt, azure-templates-variables.yml, and
    SkiaSharp.Build.props updated for the 3.132.0 version line.

Original community contribution by @ramezgerges, with follow-up
fixes by @mattleibow.

- \cx — control character (x ^ 0x40)
- \N{name} — named Unicode character via Character.codePointOf()

tag → gh release create → zip 3 tools → upload assets → cleanup.

The stale GH_TOKEN issue: fterm.exe spawns pwsh with -NoProfile so
profiles never run, and the bad token in fterm's process env is
inherited by every child shell. The script clears it on line 1.

Usage: .\release.ps1 v1.0.X

fix: Beta bug fixes and version bump to 0.1.1-beta (#51)

* Fix 8 bugs identified during beta bug hunt

Critical:
- Fix IndexPathUtils.exists()/remove() using wrong base path (storagePath
  vs contextUser.storagePath) — remove() was deleting the wrong directory,
  leaving index data orphaned
- Fix staging table crash-and-retry corruption by deduplicating on filename
  before Delta MERGE in consolidateMainStaging()

High:
- Fix AriadneJoinRule temporal dedup using join columns instead of temporal
  columns — caused different results depending on which optimizer path fired
- Fix IndexLock.readLock() wrapping Gson null result in Some() — change
  Some(gson.fromJson(...)) to Option(gson.fromJson(...)) to prevent NPE

Medium:
- Add null guard for indexes field in IndexMetadata.apply() migration chain
- Add positive-value validation for lockTimeout and lockRetryInterval
- Add positive-value validation for indexRepartitionCount
- Filter null filenames in AriadneScan file collection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix H2, H5, M9: exploded field collision, joinDf validation, remove() resilience

H2: Exploded field indexes from the same array column (e.g., users.id as
user_id and users.name as user_name) caused AMBIGUOUS_REFERENCE errors
during build. Storage columns now use as_column names instead of
array_column names, giving each field a unique Delta column. Updated
storageColumns, buildExplodedFieldIndexes, autoBloomEligibleColumns,
mapJoinColumnsToStorage, and analyzeFiles accordingly. The locateFiles
Map API now uses as_column names (e.g., 'user_id') instead of
array_column names (e.g., 'users').

H5: joinDf silently returned empty results when all join columns existed
in the schema but had no indexes. Now throws IllegalArgumentException
with a clear message listing the unindexed columns.

M9: IndexPathUtils.remove() threw FileListNotFoundException when the
file list didn't exist, preventing deletion of the storage directory.
Now catches exceptions from FileList.remove() and proceeds with
directory deletion.

* feat: auto-migrate pre-0.1.1 exploded field column names

Old indexes stored exploded field values under array_column names (e.g.,
'users'). Since 0.1.1, they are stored under as_column names (e.g.,
'user_id'). This migration detects old-style Delta columns on first
index read and rewrites the table with renamed columns. Large index
directories are also renamed.

The migration is idempotent and fast when not needed (single schema
check with a boolean flag). New indexes are unaffected.

Routing: Fixes GetOverlappingRanges CPU overhead from repeated JSON deserialization (#5748)

> 🤖 This PR was generated with the help of GitHub Copilot CLI agent.
---
## Motivation

Fixes #5747

`CollectionRoutingMap.GetOverlappingRanges` was spending excessive CPU
time in `JsonSerializable.GetValue<T>` due to repeated access of
`PartitionKeyRange.MinInclusive`. Each property access on
`PartitionKeyRange` triggers `JToken.ToObject<T>()` because the base
class `JsonSerializable` deserializes on every call with no caching.

A customer-reported PerfView trace showed **~14.8 seconds exclusive
CPU** in `GetOverlappingRanges` → `GetValue` out of a 21-minute trace
window.

## Changes

**`CollectionRoutingMap.cs`**:
Replaced `this.OrderedPartitionKeyRanges[i].MinInclusive` (triggers JSON
deserialization on every access) with `this.orderedRanges[i].Min`
(already-cached string materialized during construction).

**`CollectionRoutingMapBenchmark.cs`** (new):
Added BenchmarkDotNet benchmark for `GetOverlappingRanges` with
10/100/1000 partition counts.

## Benchmark Results

### Before (baseline on master)

| Method | PartitionCount | Mean | Allocated |
|--------|---------------|------|-----------|
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 10 | 1,115 ns | 288 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 10 | 1,221 ns | 256 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 100 | 14,331 ns | 1,328 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 100 | 24,717 ns | 2,368 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 1000 | 218,005 ns | 8,640 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 1000 | 346,477 ns | 16,848 B |

### After (this PR)

| Method | PartitionCount | Mean | Allocated |
|--------|---------------|------|-----------|
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 10 | 687 ns | 288 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 10 | 809 ns | 256 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 100 | 10,746 ns | 1,328 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 100 | 19,285 ns | 2,368 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 1000 | 173,112 ns | 8,640 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 1000 | 288,649 ns | 16,848 B |

### Improvement Summary

| Method | 10 partitions | 100 partitions | 1000 partitions |
|--------|--------------|----------------|-----------------|
| **SingleRange** | **1.6x faster** | **1.3x faster** | **1.3x faster**
|
| **MultipleRanges** | **1.5x faster** | **1.3x faster** | **1.2x
faster** |

> **Note:** Further optimization by replacing the per-call
`SortedList<string, PartitionKeyRange>` allocation with index-based
dedup yields an additional **~13x improvement** (17x total vs baseline).
This is tracked as a follow-up since the partition ranges are contiguous
and sorted by construction, making the `SortedList` dedup unnecessary.

## Testing

- All 8 existing `CollectionRoutingMapTest` unit tests pass (default +
preview builds)
- New `CollectionRoutingMapBenchmark` validates performance
characteristics

Completely redrawn 160x80 map with accurate Westeros features:

Coastlines & Bays:
- The Bite: large NE bay cutting into The North coast (rows 17-33)
- Ironman's Bay: inlet on west coast (rows 47-63, cols 42-56)
- Blackwater Bay: large bay near King's Landing (rows 62-70)

- Beyond the Wall: four distinct Haunted Forest patches in the snow
- The North: Wolfswood (heavy west forest), Frostfangs NW mountains,
  Barrowlands (SW plains), central plains, The Bite bay on NE coast
- The Neck: narrow swamp corridor, ocean on both sides
- Westerlands: mountains (Casterly Rock cliffs) east of Ironman's Bay
- Iron Islands: three island groups inside Ironman's Bay
  (Pyke/Old Wyk, Harlaw, Great Wyk)
- Riverlands: heavy forest with fertile plains along the Trident
- The Vale: mountains with small coastal plains (Gulltown)
- Crownlands: plains with Blackwater Bay indentation
- Dragonstone: small island in Blackwater Bay
- The Reach: forest-heavy with fertile plains (Mander valley)
- Stormlands: plains with coastal mountains
- Red Mountains: full barrier with Prince's Pass + Boneway gaps
- Dorne: arid plains south of Red Mountains

Updated coordinates:
- Castle Black: row 14 (south of The Wall), col 90
- Winterfell: row 27 (central North plains), col 90
- White Harbor: row 26, col 115 (coast of The Bite)
- Moat Cailin: row 36 (in The Neck swamp), col 80
- Riverrun/Harrenhal/Eyrie/Casterly Rock repositioned
- King's Landing: north shore of Blackwater Bay (100, 60)
- Dragonstone: island in Blackwater Bay (108, 65)

privacy(P2-6): self-host Work Sans font — remove Google Fonts CDN

GDPR Art.6 issue: every page load previously sent the user's IP address to
Google (fonts.googleapis.com + fonts.gstatic.com) with no legal basis and
no user consent. This is a recognised GDPR violation — German and Austrian
courts have issued rulings against Google Fonts CDN use (LG München I,
Az. 3 O 17493/20).

Changes:
- Removed 3 <link> tags pointing to fonts.googleapis.com / fonts.gstatic.com
- Downloaded Work Sans v24 WOFF2 files (3 subsets, OFL 1.1 licence):
    public/fonts/work-sans-latin.woff2       (49 KB)
    public/fonts/work-sans-latin-ext.woff2   (35 KB)
    public/fonts/work-sans-vietnamese.woff2  (12 KB)
- Created public/fonts/work-sans.css with @font-face rules for all 3 weights
  (400/500/600) × 3 subsets, preserving unicode-range subsetting for
  performance (only latin file loads for Spanish content)
- index.html now loads <link rel=stylesheet href=/fonts/work-sans.css>

No external network requests are made for fonts. The app now works fully
offline after initial install.

commit.message:
feat: add breakpoint mode, debug mode, and debugger detection (#204)

- Server: breakpoint mode pauses game when a bot misses its turn
  deadline; debug mode advances one turn at a time via controller
- Server: GamePausedEvent now includes pauseCause field with values
  pause, debug_step, and breakpoint
- Server: ServerHandshake advertises debugMode and breakpointMode
  server features
- Bot APIs (Java, Python, .NET): detect attached debugger via
  ROBOCODE_DEBUG env var or native debugger check; report via
  debuggerAttached field in BotHandshake
- GUI: breakpoint toggle per bot in BotPropertiesPanel, disabled when
  server does not support breakpoint mode; debugger-attached indicator
- Client model: BotInfo and Participant expose debuggerAttached field;
  Messages expose Features, GamePausedEvent, EnableDebugMode,
  DisableDebugMode, BotPolicyUpdate
- Runner API: BattleHandle exposes serverFeatures, enableDebugMode(),
  disableDebugMode(), setBotPolicy()
- Schema: bot-handshake gains debuggerAttached field; server-handshake
  gains features object; fix required block placement in
  bot-handshake.schema.yaml
- Tests: unit and integration tests for all new runner API methods

feat(usbd480): USBD480 NX series support (#68)

* fix(usbd480): pass non-NULL buffer for zero-length OUT control transfers

WinUsb_ControlTransfer on a per-interface WinUSB composite device rejects
a NULL buffer pointer for OUT transfers even when BufferLength = 0. Despite
what MSDN documentation states, in practice WinUSB's composite interface
handling requires a valid (non-NULL) pointer for all OUT control transfers.

This matches libusb's behavior in winusbx_submit_control_transfer, which
always passes transfer->buffer + LIBUSB_CONTROL_SETUP_SIZE regardless of
whether the data length is zero.

Root cause investigation: SimHub uses USBD480_lib2.dll which embeds libusb-1.0
(WinUSB backend). libusb works because it always passes a non-NULL buffer.
Our code passed NULL + 0 for all controlOut calls (SET_ADDRESS, SET_BRIGHTNESS,
SET_FRAME_START_ADDRESS), causing every OUT transfer to fail with:
'A device attached to the system is not functioning.'

Fix: pass &dummy[0] (1-byte stack variable) as buffer with BufferLength=0.

The existing per-interface WinUSB install (Zadig -> Interface 0) is correct
and will now work without reinstalling anything.

Also updated SKILL.md with correct root cause analysis and revised Zadig
guidance: Interface 0 installs are the preferred approach (keeps HID
touchscreen active), whole-device installs also work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(usbd480): wake device from USB selective suspend on open

When SimHub disables the USBD480 screen and releases its handle, the WinUSB
driver leaves the device in AUTO_SUSPEND mode. WinUsb_Initialize succeeds
(the interface can be opened) but all subsequent control transfers fail with
ERROR_GEN_FAILURE because the USB device is still suspended at the bus level.

Fix: call WinUsb_SetPowerPolicy(AUTO_SUSPEND=FALSE) immediately after
WinUsb_Initialize. Per the Windows docs, setting AUTO_SUSPEND=FALSE on a
suspended device causes the host to bring it back to full power immediately,
making all subsequent transfers work.

Also adds procWinUsbSetPowerPolicy to the shared WinUSB proc declarations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(usbd480): use RECIP_DEVICE (0x40/0xC0) for NX series control transfers

The NX series firmware (PID 0x08A7) expects USB_RECIP_DEVICE in bmRequestType,
not USB_RECIP_INTERFACE like the old WQ43 (PID 0x08A6). Sending RECIP_INTERFACE
(0x41/0xC1) causes the NX firmware to STALL the endpoint immediately, which
Windows reports as ERROR_GEN_FAILURE (31) on every single control transfer —
both IN and OUT — regardless of the buffer or other parameters.

Confirmed via diagnostic test program:
  0xC1 (IN|VENDOR|IFACE)  → FAILED for GET_DEVICE_DETAILS
  0xC0 (IN|VENDOR|DEVICE) → OK: returns 'USBD480-NX50' + 800x480
  0x41 (OUT|VENDOR|IFACE) → FAILED for SET_BRIGHTNESS
  0x40 (OUT|VENDOR|DEVICE)→ OK

Fix: usbd480ReqTypeOut 0x41→0x40, usbd480ReqTypeIn 0xC1→0xC0

Also updates SKILL.md with the confirmed NX bmRequestType values and adds
the RECIP_INTERFACE failure mode to the Common Failure Modes table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove CFSClean from release pipeline to unblock NuGet publishing (#15974)

The CFSClean network isolation policy blocks all NuGet endpoints
(api.nuget.org, www.nuget.org, etc.) via DNS sinkhole to 192.0.2.5.
This causes the 1ES.PublishNuget@1 task in the release pipeline to fail
with 'SocketException 10013: An attempt was made to access a socket in a
way forbidden by its access permissions' when trying to push packages to
NuGet.org.

ES4.2.4 requires pipelines to *consume* packages from internal feeds
(which we already do - NuGet.config uses <clear/> and only references
pkgs.dev.azure.com/dnceng feeds), but does not prohibit *publishing* to
public registries. CFSClean2 is retained to continue blocking Docker,
PowerShell Gallery, and other endpoints not needed by this pipeline.

The main build pipeline (azure-pipelines.yml) correctly keeps CFSClean
since it only restores from internal feeds.

Failing build: https://dev.azure.com/dnceng/internal/_build/results?buildId=2946410

ci: consolidate workflows, add manual release trigger (#18)

* ci: consolidate workflows into single Build pipeline with manual release

Merge ci.yml, lint.yml, and security.yml into build-images.yml as
parallel jobs (lint, test, govulncheck) that run alongside image builds
on every PR and push to main.

Add workflow_dispatch trigger for manual releases:
- Select version bump level (patch/minor/major)
- Optional pre-release and dry-run flags
- Auto-computes next version from latest tag
- Creates and pushes the tag
- Release job depends on all checks passing (lint, test, govulncheck,
  image builds, manifest rendering, plugin builds)

Remove tag-push trigger -- releases are now a deliberate action via
the GitHub Actions UI, not triggered by pushing tags.

Update copilot instructions to reflect the new release process.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: rename build-images.yml to ci.yml

The workflow now contains lint, test, security scan, image builds,
and release -- 'Build' no longer describes it accurately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add fmt check and codegen validation to lint job

The lint job now verifies:
- gofmt: fails if any files are not formatted
- make generate: fails if generated code (DeepCopy, CRDs) is stale
- golangci-lint: unchanged (includes govet internally)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add go mod tidy check to lint job

Fails if go.mod or go.sum are not tidy, catching forgotten
go mod tidy after dependency changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add pre-commit hook and update contributing guide

Add dev/pre-commit hook that verifies gofmt on staged Go files.
Install via: ln -sf ../../dev/pre-commit .git/hooks/pre-commit

Update README Contributing section with pre-commit hook setup,
full lint/test steps, changelog requirement, and CI note.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: move pre-commit hook to scripts/ (dev/ is gitignored)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add draft option for releases (default: true)

New workflow_dispatch input 'draft' (boolean, default true) controls
whether the release is created as a draft. Defaults to draft so
releases can be reviewed before publishing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: rename manifest job for clarity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: restructure pipeline into 4 sequential stages

Stage 1 (parallel): version, lint, test, govulncheck
Stage 2 (parallel, after stage 1): build (amd64/arm64), plugin (x6)
Stage 3 (parallel, after stage 2): build manifests, multi-arch images
Stage 4 (after stage 3): create draft release

Previously build ran in parallel with lint/test, so a failing test
would not block image pushes. Now all checks must pass before any
build artifacts are produced.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: remove unnecessary frontend build from assets job

The render-manifests tool only templates YAML deploy files and has no
dependency on pkg/html or the embedded frontend. The frontend build
was carried over from when this was a combined job.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove accidentally committed binary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: deduplicate version metadata into single version job

The version, commit, build_time, sha_short, and image_tags were
computed redundantly in 4 separate jobs. Move all metadata to the
version job outputs and reference them via needs.version.outputs
throughout the pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: update Node.js from 20 to 24

Node.js 20 is deprecated on GitHub Actions runners and will be
removed September 2026. Update all setup-node steps to version 24.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: enable Go module cache on lint job

The lint job had cache: false on setup-go, originally to avoid
conflicts with golangci-lint's own cache. However, the gofmt, go mod
tidy, and make generate steps all benefit from cached modules.
golangci-lint works correctly with setup-go caching enabled.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: remove push-to-main trigger, images only built on release

Since direct pushes to main are not allowed, the push trigger only
fired on PR merges. With the workflow_dispatch release flow, images
are only needed during releases. This eliminates the duplicate CI
run on merge and avoids pushing dev images to the registry.

Image push and multi-arch manifest creation now only happen on
workflow_dispatch (manual release).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: normalize job dependencies for consistent execution graph

All jobs explicitly list their full dependency chain so the GitHub
Actions UI renders a clean stage-by-stage graph:

Stage 1: version -> lint, test, govulncheck (parallel)
Stage 2: build (x2 arch), plugin (x6) (after stage 1)
Stage 3: manifest, assets (after stage 2)
Stage 4: release (after stage 3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: rename Build Manifests to Render Kubernetes Manifests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: replace govulncheck-action with direct invocation

The golang/govulncheck-action wraps govulncheck in a Node.js action
runner that uses Node.js 20, triggering deprecation warnings. Replace
with a direct go install + govulncheck invocation which has no
Node.js dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Video Indexer, Multi-Modal Enhancements, Scope Bug

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled
- Validate image upload UI shows extracted text and vision analysis
- Confirm conversation history properly handles image context without token overflow

### 🔐 Security & Performance
- Managed identity authentication improves security posture (no stored API keys)
- Image base64 data excluded from conversation history prevents token exhaustion
- Metadata citations add minimal overhead while providing rich context
- Vision analysis runs efficiently during document processing pipeline

### 📝 Configuration Required
Admins must configure:
1. Enable `enable_multimodal_vision` in admin settings
2. Select vision-capable model (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision-preview`)
3. For Video Indexer: Configure managed identity and ARM resource details
4. Enable `enable_extract_meta_data` to surface metadata citations

---

This PR significantly enhances the application's multi-modal capabilities, providing users with richer context from images and documents while maintaining efficient token usage and robust security practices.

1. Pin Conversations
Users can pin important conversations to keep them at the top of the list
Pinned conversations display a pin icon (📌) in the conversation header and details modal
Pin icon appears before the conversation title

2. Hide Conversations
Users can hide conversations to declutter their workspace without deleting them
Hidden conversations display an eye-slash icon (👁️‍🗨️) in the conversation header and details modal
Eye-slash icon appears next to the pin icon (if pinned)
Bulk hide/unhide operations available in multi-select mode

Date range (from/to)
Chat type (personal/group/public)
Classifications (multi-select)
Has uploaded files
Has generated images
Pagination (20 results per page)
Message snippets with highlighted search terms (50 chars before/after match)

Smooth scroll animation to navigate to specific messages

Pin/unpin selected conversations
Hide/unhide selected conversations
Delete selected conversations

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled
- Validate image upload UI shows extracted text and vision analysis
- Confirm conversation history properly handles image context without token overflow

### 🔐 Security & Performance
- Managed identity authentication improves security posture (no stored API keys)
- Image base64 data excluded from conversation history prevents token exhaustion
- Metadata citations add minimal overhead while providing rich context
- Vision analysis runs efficiently during document processing pipeline

### 📝 Configuration Required
Admins must configure:
1. Enable `enable_multimodal_vision` in admin settings
2. Select vision-capable model (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision-preview`)
3. For Video Indexer: Configure managed identity and ARM resource details
4. Enable `enable_extract_meta_data` to surface metadata citations

---

This PR significantly enhances the application's multi-modal capabilities, providing users with richer context from images and documents while maintaining efficient token usage and robust security practices.

1. Pin Conversations
Users can pin important conversations to keep them at the top of the list
Pinned conversations display a pin icon (📌) in the conversation header and details modal
Pin icon appears before the conversation title
Bulk pin/unpin operations available in multi-select mode
Pinned conversations always appear first, sorted by most recent activity
2. Hide Conversations
Users can hide conversations to declutter their workspace without deleting them
Hidden conversations display an eye-slash icon (👁️‍🗨️) in the conversation header and details modal
Eye-slash icon appears next to the pin icon (if pinned)
Bulk hide/unhide operations available in multi-select mode
Toggle visibility of hidden conversations using the eye icon in the sidebar
3. Two-Tier Conversation Search
Quick Search (Sidebar)
Instant title-based filtering of conversations
Search icon in sidebar activates inline search input
Real-time filtering as you type
Clear button to reset search
Expand button to open advanced search modal
Advanced Search (Modal)
Full-text search across all message content
Multiple filter options:
Date range (from/to)
Chat type (personal/group/public)
Classifications (multi-select)
Has uploaded files
Has generated images
Pagination (20 results per page)
Message snippets with highlighted search terms (50 chars before/after match)
Click to navigate directly to specific messages
Search history tracking (last 20 searches)
Clickable search history to repeat searches
4. Message Highlighting & Navigation
Search results highlight matched text in yellow (amber in dark mode)
Smooth scroll animation to navigate to specific messages
Pulse animation draws attention to the target message
Highlights persist for 30 seconds before auto-clearing
Works across conversation switches
5. Multi-Select Mode
Select multiple conversations for bulk operations
Visual checkboxes appear when entering selection mode
Bulk actions available:
Pin/unpin selected conversations
Hide/unhide selected conversations
Delete selected conversations
Selection mode accessible from conversation dropdown menu
Auto-exit after 30 seconds of inactivity
6. Enhanced Conversation Details Modal
Displays pin icon if conversation is pinned
Displays eye-slash icon if conversation is hidden
Shows both icons at the top of the modal (next to title)
Status section shows visual badges for pinned/hidden state
Comprehensive metadata display
Technical Implementation
Frontend Changes
chat-conversations.js: Core conversation management, quick search, pin/hide functionality
chat-search-modal.js (NEW): Advanced search modal implementation
chat-sidebar-conversations.js: Sidebar search synchronization, hidden conversation handling
chat-messages.js: Message highlighting, smooth scroll, search highlight persistence
chat-conversation-details.js: Updated to show pin/hidden icons in modal
chats.css: Styles for search highlights and message pulse animations
HTML Templates: Added search modal, updated navigation icons
Backend Changes
route_backend_conversations.py:

/api/search_conversations - Full-text search with filters and pagination
/api/conversations/classifications - Get unique classification values
/api/user-settings/search-history - GET/POST/DELETE endpoints for search history
/api/conversations/{id}/pin - Toggle pin status
/api/conversations/{id}/hide - Toggle hide status
Bulk operations for pin/hide/delete

commit.message:
Support \p{java...} character classes (fixes #106) (#110)

Add support for all 18 java.lang.Character predicate-based character
classes (\p{javaLowerCase}, \p{javaUpperCase}, \p{javaWhitespace},
\p{javaMirrored}, \p{javaAlphabetic}, \p{javaIdeographic},
\p{javaTitleCase}, \p{javaDigit}, \p{javaDefined}, \p{javaLetter},
\p{javaLetterOrDigit}, \p{javaSpaceChar}, \p{javaISOControl},
\p{javaIdentifierIgnorable}, \p{javaUnicodeIdentifierStart},
\p{javaUnicodeIdentifierPart}, \p{javaJavaIdentifierStart},
\p{javaJavaIdentifierPart}).

Range tables are computed at class-load time from the running JVM's
Character methods, so they always match the JVM's Unicode version.

Fixes #106

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
